decks.notes: The main concept of this UR Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with low-cost aggressive creatures and burn spells. By utilizing cards like Soul-Scar Mage, Monastery Swiftspear, and Emberheart Challenger, along with burn spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage, the goal is to consistently apply pressure to the opponent's life total and clear the way for attacks. The deck also includes cards like Academic Dispute and Stormchaser's Talent to help maintain card advantage and provide additional damage. Overall, the strategy is to quickly close out the game before the opponent can stabilize.

This deck focuses on assembling the Greasefang Parhelion combo, utilizing cards like Guardian of New Benalia, Monument to Endurance, and Parhelion II. The strategy is to protect and support the combo pieces with cards like Sheltered by Ghosts and Lively Dirge while disrupting the opponent with cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push. The mana base includes a mix of lands to support the colors needed for the combo. Overall, the deck aims to quickly set up the combo and overwhelm the opponent with powerful synergies.

This deck focuses on utilizing Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II to create a powerful combo that can quickly overwhelm opponents. By using cards like Fleeting Spirit, Monument to Endurance, and Iron-Shield Elf to protect and support the combo pieces, the deck aims to control the battlefield and disrupt the opponent's strategies. With cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to remove threats and disrupt the opponent's hand, this deck seeks to build a strong board presence and secure victory through its powerful combo interactions.

The main focus of this UW Control deck is to control the board through counterspells, removal, and card advantage. The deck aims to consistently disrupt the opponent's strategy while slowly building up its own resources and win conditions. The addition of Kaheera, the Orphanguard as a companion adds an additional element of synergy and flexibility to the deck. By efficiently managing resources and utilizing powerful spells like Teferi, Supreme Verdict, and March of Otherworldly Light, this deck aims to outlast and outplay opponents in a grindy, control-heavy matchup.

The deck focuses on using Niv-Mizzet Reborn as the central control piece, utilizing a wide variety of multicolored cards to control the board and disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck includes a mix of removal spells, ramp spells, and versatile threats to answer different types of threats and maintain board presence. By leveraging the diverse array of cards available in Pioneer, the deck aims to outvalue and outlast the opponent through incremental advantage and powerful card selection.

This deck focuses on utilizing Niv-Mizzet Reborn as a powerful control strategy, with a variety of versatile spells and creatures to support it. The deck combines a mix of removal spells, creature threats, and card advantage engines to outvalue opponents and maintain control of the game. The addition of Yorion, Sky Nomad as a companion allows for additional value and flexibility in longer games. Overall, the key concept of this deck is to adapt to various situations, control the board, and generate card advantage to outmaneuver opponents in Pioneer 1 vs 1 matches.

This red aggro deck focuses on overwhelming the opponent with quick, aggressive creatures and burn spells. The key strategy is to play low-cost creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage to deal damage quickly, while also using burn spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage to remove any blockers or threats from the opponent. The deck also utilizes powerful creatures like Kumano Faces Kakkazan for additional damage and control, making it a fast and efficient deck that aims to win the game before the opponent can stabilize.

The deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with aggressive creatures and burn spells to deal damage directly to the opponent. Using cards like Monastery Swiftspear, Soul-Scar Mage, and Emberheart Challenger, along with burn spells like Burst Lightning and Lightning Strike, the deck aims to end the game quickly before the opponent has a chance to stabilize. The inclusion of Den of the Bugbear and Ramunap Ruins provides additional reach in the late game, while cards like Reckless Rage and Monstrous Rage help clear the way for attacks.

This deck focuses on assembling the Greasefang Parhelion combo by ramping with Monument to Endurance and using Fleeting Spirit to generate tokens. The key strategy is to protect the combo pieces with Iron-Shield Elf and Guardian of New Benalia while disrupting the opponent's game plan with Thoughtseize and Duress. Parhelion II acts as the finisher, providing aerial pressure and closing out the game. The deck also utilizes removal spells like Fatal Push and Vanishing Verse to control the board and ensure the combo can go off smoothly.

This UR Aggro deck focuses on quickly dealing damage to the opponent through a combination of creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Emberheart Challenger, as well as burn spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage. The deck also runs disruptive spells like Academic Dispute and Boomerang Basics to control the board and keep the pressure on the opponent. The key strategy is to be aggressive and maintain a steady stream of threats while disrupting the opponent's game plan.

This deck focuses on playing aggressive creatures early on to apply pressure on the opponent while disrupting their game plan with discard spells like Thoughtseize and Duress. The deck also utilizes removal spells like Fatal Push and Go for the Throat to deal with opposing threats. The combination of efficient creatures, disruption, and removal allows the deck to maintain board control and close out the game quickly. The inclusion of Mutavault provides additional creature threats that can dodge sorcery-speed removal. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a well-rounded aggressive strategy.

This deck revolves around assembling the combo of Greasefang and Parhelion II to create a powerful synergy that can overwhelm opponents. The deck includes a mix of control elements such as Thoughtseize and Fatal Push, along with creatures like Iron-Shield Elf and Fleeting Spirit to help protect the combo pieces. Monument to Endurance also provides a way to recur key cards if they are destroyed. The mana base is carefully constructed to support the deck's requirements while also allowing for flexibility in casting spells.

This deck focuses on using powerful control elements such as Thoughtseize, Abrupt Decay, and Fatal Push to disrupt the opponent's strategy while gradually building up a strong board presence with creatures like Sentinel of the Nameless City and Badgermole Cub. The goal is to maintain board control and outvalue the opponent through efficient removal spells and resilient threats like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse. The inclusion of lands like Boseiju, Who Endures and Takenuma, Abandoned Mire provides additional utility and flexibility in dealing with various threats and situations.

This deck focuses on controlling the game by disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Duress and Thoughtseize, while also having removal spells like Fatal Push and Abrupt Decay to deal with threats. It also aims to generate value through creatures like Sentinel of the Nameless City and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse. The deck utilizes lands like Mutavault and Overgrown Tomb to ramp and fix mana. The inclusion of cards like Culling Ritual and Bitter Triumph helps in maintaining board control and out-valuing the opponent.

The main focus of this Selesnya Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with fast and efficient creatures and disruption spells. By using mana dorks like Elvish Mystic and Llanowar Elves to ramp into powerful threats like Archon of Emeria and Skyclave Apparition, the deck aims to establish board control early on and maintain pressure throughout the game. Collected Company allows for explosive turns by putting multiple creatures into play at once, while cards like Thalia, Guardian of Thraben and Aven Interrupter disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck also includes some card advantage and protection with cards like Michelangelo's Technique and Toski, Bearer of Secrets.

This UR Aggro deck focuses on utilizing cheap spells and creatures to quickly deal damage to the opponent. Academic Dispute can give card advantage while also dealing damage, Boomerang Basics can bounce problematic creatures or lands, and Reckless Rage can remove threats while also triggering prowess abilities. Monastery Swiftspear, Emberheart Challenger, and Soul-Scar Mage are all low-cost creatures that can quickly grow in power. The deck aims to be fast and aggressive, utilizing burn spells and efficient creatures to overwhelm the opponent before they can stabilize.
